Name: ODD
LongName: Overview, Design concepts, and Details (ODD) protocol for describing agent-based and other simulation models
Description: ODD is a structured protocol for transparent, complete, and replicable documentation of agent-based and related simulation models, covering model purpose, entities, processes, design concepts, and implementation details.
Aim: To standardize simulation-model reporting so readers can understand model logic, assess fitness for purpose, reproduce implementations, and compare models across disciplines.

Scope.Coverage: Simulation-model manuscripts that document model structure, mechanisms, and implementation details, especially agent-based or individual-based models but also other simulation models using ODD-style structure.
Scope.Focus: Clear reporting of model purpose, represented entities and state variables, process scheduling, key design concepts, initialization, input data dependencies, and submodel formulations sufficient for replication and critical appraisal.
Scope.ContrastWith: Not intended for purely statistical analyses or non-simulation empirical studies. Complements, rather than replaces, design-specific reporting standards by adding a simulation-model documentation layer.

primary.description: Grimm V, Railsback SF, Vincenot CE, et al. The ODD Protocol for Describing Agent-Based and Other Simulation Models: A Second Update to Improve Clarity, Replication, and Structural Realism. Journal of Artificial Societies and Social Simulation. 2020;23(2):7.

items.item_question: Does the manuscript state model purpose and key patterns used for model evaluation?
items.item_question_background: Purpose anchors model scope and patterns support fitness-for-purpose evaluation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the explicit model purpose, intended use, and any empirical or theoretical patterns used to evaluate model behavior.

source_page_refs.quote: In its first element '1. Purpose and patterns' the purpose of the model and the patterns that serve as model evaluation criteria are briefly described.

items.review_reason: Extracted text includes OCR artifacts; verify exact wording against source PDF.

items.item_question: Are entities, their state variables, and temporal/spatial scales fully specified?
items.item_question_background: Explicit entities and scales are required to interpret model structure and results.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all entity types, associated state variables, spatial resolution/extent, and temporal resolution/extent.

source_page_refs.quote: Entities, state variables and scales lists the different types of entities represented in the model... The temporal and spatial resolution and extent...

items.review_reason: OCR-cleanup needed for exact entity/scale phrasing.

items.item_question: Is there a clear overview of processes, update order, and scheduling?
items.item_question_background: Execution order and update timing strongly influence simulation outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract process sequence, actor/process ownership, timing/frequency, and state-update schedule details.

source_page_refs.quote: Process overview and scheduling provides an overview of the processes in the model: Which entities do what, at what time and in what order...

items.review_reason: Verify extracted scheduling terms against clean PDF rendering.

items.item_question: Are the basic theoretical or conceptual principles underlying the model stated?
items.item_question_background: Conceptual grounding helps evaluate whether model structure matches domain theory.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract domain theories, principles, or assumptions that motivated the model’s core structure.

items.item_question: Are emergent outcomes and how they arise from lower-level processes described?
items.item_question_background: Emergence is a central rationale for many ABMs and should be explicit.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract emergent patterns/outcomes and the mechanisms expected to generate them.

items.item_question: Is adaptation represented and clearly explained if relevant?
items.item_question_background: Adaptive behavior is often a key design choice affecting predictive behavior.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract adaptive rules, triggers, and how agent behavior changes over time or context.

items.item_question: Are agent-level objectives or optimization criteria documented where applicable?
items.item_question_background: Explicit objectives clarify decision logic and model behavior.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract stated agent goals, utilities, objective functions, or decision criteria.

items.item_question: If learning is modeled, are learning mechanisms and memory effects described?
items.item_question_background: Learning can alter trajectories and should be reproducibly defined.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract learning update rules, memory structures, and learning parameters.

items.item_question: Does the model include anticipatory behavior, and is this mechanism reported?
items.item_question_background: Prediction/anticipation mechanisms can materially affect agent decisions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract how agents form forecasts or anticipatory estimates and how these are used.

items.item_question: Are sensing/perception assumptions and information availability described?
items.item_question_background: Perception constraints define agent information sets and feasible decisions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract what information entities can observe, sensing range, and perception limitations.

items.item_question: Are interaction mechanisms among entities and environment specified?
items.item_question_background: Interaction structure determines system dynamics and emergent outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract interaction rules, network/spatial interaction constraints, and interaction frequencies.

items.item_question: Are stochastic components, distributions, and randomization controls reported?
items.item_question_background: Randomness handling is critical for reproducibility and uncertainty characterization.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract stochastic elements, random distributions, random seed policies, and replication settings.

items.item_question: If collectives/groups are represented, are their formation and dynamics described?
items.item_question_background: Collective structures can create important non-linear effects.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract rules for group/collective formation, maintenance, and dissolution.

items.item_question: Are virtual observation and output-measurement procedures described?
items.item_question_background: Observation design affects comparability to empirical data and evaluation outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract what outputs are recorded, aggregation procedures, and links to empirical observation equivalents.

items.item_question: Are initial conditions and starting state assignment procedures fully described?
items.item_question_background: Initialization can strongly influence transients and long-run behavior.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract initial state setup, entity initialization values, initial distributions, and any burn-in setup.

source_page_refs.quote: The final three elements '5. Initialization', '6. Input data', and '7. Submodels' provide detail sufficient...

items.review_reason: Element heading was extracted with OCR artifacts; verify exact formatting.

items.item_question: Are all exogenous inputs and data dependencies specified?
items.item_question_background: External inputs determine scenario behavior and model reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all external data sources, update cadence, preprocessing, and how inputs feed model components.

source_page_refs.quote: The final three elements '5. Initialization', '6. Input data', and '7. Submodels' provide detail sufficient...

items.review_reason: OCR noise present; confirm against source PDF.

items.item_question: Are submodels and process-level formulations documented in enough detail for reimplementation?
items.item_question_background: Reimplementation requires explicit equations, rules, and algorithmic detail.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract each submodel with equations/rules, parameters, and execution context links to scheduling.

source_page_refs.quote: The final three elements ... provide detail sufficient for readers to fully understand the model and ... re-implement it.

items.review_reason: Verify reimplementation language due OCR conversion artifacts.

items.item_question: Is model rationale documented to explain major structural and design choices?
items.item_question_background: Rationale supports structural realism and interpretability of design decisions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract explicit justification for key design choices, abstraction decisions, and inclusion/exclusion of mechanisms.

source_page_refs.quote: ...lack of provision for sections ... covering model design rationale...

items.review_reason: Rationale section is part of update guidance; check exact section naming in source.

items.item_question: Are methods and criteria for evaluating model fitness for purpose clearly described?
items.item_question_background: Evaluation criteria are needed to judge validity and intended use.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract evaluation strategy, calibration/validation checks, fit criteria, and interpretation limits.

source_page_refs.quote: ...means by which the model’s fitness for purpose is evaluated.

items.review_reason: Evaluation terminology extracted from abstract; verify exact protocol phrasing.

Aliases: The ODD Protocol for Describing Agent-Based and Other Simulation Models
EvidenceExamplesPositive: The model is documented following ODD with sections for Purpose and patterns, Entities/state variables/scales, Process overview and scheduling, Design concepts, Initialization, Input data, and Submodels.
We provide an ODD-style ABM description including adaptation, stochasticity, sensing and interaction assumptions, plus submodel equations to support reimplementation.
This simulation methods paper reports model rationale, process scheduling, and fitness-for-purpose evaluation criteria alongside full submodel details.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: The study reports odds ratios from logistic regression but contains no simulation model description framework.
A clinical paper discusses unusual or odd symptoms and has no agent-based or simulation model documentation.
The manuscript presents a statistical analysis workflow without entities, scheduling, or submodel definitions.
Matching.Explain: Apply as a reporting overlay when manuscripts describe or should describe simulation-model structure and implementation in ODD-like form, especially agent-based and individual-based models where full-text method sections contain the strongest cues.
